reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: My puzzle is 95 - 61 = 34, simple arithmetic. My track record with immediate volunteering is now 7 successes vs 1 failure (88% success rate). The expected value calculation continues to strongly favor volunteering: ~85-90% × 4 coins = 3.4-3.6 EV vs. passing with 25% random death risk per round (~2.25 EV). Four consecutive successful games (7, 8, 9, 10) confirm the strategy works reliably. These are basic arithmetic puzzles where errors are rare. Volunteering immediately remains the optimal play.
reasoning.B:

Three of four capsules get independent cross-verification, with only Q3 as a single-endorsement risk on simple math. D never spends coins to verify others, and Q4 was the exact failure point in Game 5. Spending 1 coin (4→3) to verify D's work remains the highest-value investment. No reason to deviate from a proven winning pattern.
